title: Mass yield distribution of target residues from the reaction of indium with 42 MeV/nucleon 12C ions

x4freetext: The irradiation was performed on heavy
The target assembly used for the irradiation
consisted of three pieces of 99.99% pure natural indium
foils (95.3% 115In), backed by 1.8 mg/cm2 aluminum foil
and their aluminum or Mylar catcher foils.
The thickness of these targets was around 18 mg/cm2.
The irradiation lasted for about 10 hours, with
typical beam intensities of 5-10 electrons nanoampere.
The total particle fluence was 1.9E+14 12C ions.
At the end of the irradiation, the middle
indium target together with its Mylar catcher foil,
10.4 mg/cm2 thick, was analyzed by direct
The other two indium targets with their
aluminium catcher foils were subjected to chemical
separations. The chemical procedures used in this work
were based mainly on the differences in the
solubilities of sulfides. The procedures yield six
The counting of the samples began about 23 min
[for unseparated foils] or about 40 min [for chemical
fractions] after the end of irradiation and continued
Decay curves were analyzed using a
calculation of the production cross sections were done
- By compiler. The authors did not give any decay data
* The uncertainty in the thickness of the targets is
* The uncertainties in beam-current measurement and in
the chemical yields are not included.
The errors listed are the standard deviations,
including the errors in analysis of gamma spectra and
in resolution of the decay curves by the least-squares
